meaning: adj.切去顶端的,缩短了的,被删节的
examples: My article was published in truncated form.
The review body has produced a truncated version of its annual report.
The output in the listing has been truncated.
In some cases, the bidding period was truncated from five days to thirteen hours.
Critical modernization programs, particularly in the Air Force, have been truncated, delayed, or reduced in scope.
